mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 returns within 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.3pp threshold over a 24-month out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day forward returns in over 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in quarterly regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply-side bottlenecks that compress profit margins and cause rapid negative S&P 500 price adjustments within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat during industrial production contractions, with a negative return hit rate below 40% over a 36-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a stationary or rising federal funds rate creates a real rate squeeze that erodes equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation and high-rate regime generates positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, invalidating the real-rate squeeze mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases household income and consumption capacity, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show a negative or zero correlation with payroll growth across multiple 12-month rolling windows, with Rank IC consistently below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts below 650,000 units signals a collapse in construction demand and related supply chains, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in over 65% of months where housing starts drop below the 650,000 threshold, demonstrating complete signal breakdown.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and retail sales growth indicates inventory accumulation that forces corporate markdowns, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The supply-demand divergence signal fails to predict negative returns, with 20-day forward returns exceeding zero in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 3-year horizon.
